title: Combine Scientific Forecasts with Indigenous Knowledge
description: Warnings should include both meteorological information and observations from local elders. When both sources point to the same outcome, communities are more likely to trust the message.
responsible: NDMA, KMD, and county government EWS teams
addresses_barriers: Distrust and Unreliability of Formal Forecasts
Competing Knowledge Systems and Trust Dynamics

title: Provide Clear Action Guidance on EWS Channels
description: Warnings should explain the expected severity of the risk and outline practical steps communities can take, such as livestock movement, accessing support services, or preparing resources.
responsible: NDMA, KMD, and county government communication officers
addresses_barriers: Poor Timeliness and Lack of Action Guidance

title: Use Radio and Community Meetings as the Primary Warning Channels
description: Many pastoralists do not have reliable internet or smartphones. Radio broadcasts and meetings organised through local leaders are more accessible and trusted.
responsible: NDMA, KMD, the county government, and NGOs operating in Samburu County

title: Communicate Warnings in Local Languages and Accessible Formats
description: Warnings should be delivered in Samburu and Kiswahili and supported with voice messages, pictures, and colour-coded indicators that clearly show levels of risk.
responsible: NDMA, KMD, county government communication teams, and NGO field officers

title: Establish a Joint Warning Validation Committee
description: A local committee including elders, chiefs, and government representatives could meet regularly to review forecasts, compare them with local observations, and agree on a shared message.
responsible: County government, NDMA, and community leadership structures including councils of elders and chiefs
addresses_barriers: Distrust and Unreliability of Formal Forecasts

title: Establish a Shared Resource Pool for Early Response
description: Communities often know a drought is coming but lack the means to respond. Pre-positioned resources such as livestock feed or water support could allow earlier action before conditions worsen.
responsible: County government, NDMA, and development partners and NGOs funding drought preparedness in Samburu County

title: Ensure Women Are Included in Warning Dissemination Pathways
description: Women play a major role in household food security but are often excluded from information channels. Deliberately sharing warnings through women's groups and community networks could improve overall preparedness.
responsible: NGOs, community leaders, chiefs, and local government officers responsible for community outreach in Samburu County
